cards.grammarNote: أَيْنَ (ayna) = where. In equational questions (no verb), the question word is followed directly by the subject: أَيْنَ الْحَمَّامُ؟ (lit. 'where the bathroom?'). No verb needed.

cards.front: How do I get to the station?
cards.grammarNote: كَيْفَ (kayfa) = how. Followed by a verb: كَيْفَ + verb phrase. أَصِلُ (aṣilu) = I reach/get to, from the verb وَصَلَ (waṣala). Note إِلَى (ilā) = to, for destinations.
مَتَى (matā) = when. Used for questions about time. Not to be confused with مَا (mā) = what.
لِمَاذَا (limādhā) = why (lit. 'for what'). لِ (li) = for/because of + مَاذَا (mādhā) = what. In speech, لِيه (lēh) is the colloquial equivalent heard in Egyptian Arabic.
مَنْ (man) = who. Used for people. Do not confuse with مِن (min) = from (different vowel). مَنْ هَذَا؟ = who is this? مَنْ أَنْت؟ = who are you?
مَا (mā) = what (for definitions/identities). مَاذَا (mādhā) = what (for actions with verbs): مَاذَا تَفْعَل؟ (what are you doing?). مَا هَذَا؟ = what is this?
كَمْ (kam) = how much / how many / how far. Used for quantity and distance. كَمْ يَكْلُف؟ = how much does it cost? كَمْ الْمَسَافَة؟ = how far is the distance?

cards.front: Which bus goes to the center?
cards.grammarNote: أَيّ (ayy) = which/what kind of. It is followed by a noun in the nominative. أَيُّ (with nominative ending -u) before the noun: أَيُّ حَافِلَة = which bus.
